acceptance_targets: [CH15-BOOKER-001] Pass if, on reread of the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:213, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:363, each confrontation turns on a different leverage point and produces a different immediate outcome; fail if two or more exchanges still hinge on the same cost/obligation argument.

[CH15-CRAFT-001] Pass if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:159 contains explicit subject-plus-finite-verb sentence structure (outside quoted lists/dialogue); fail if stand-alone clipped fragments remain in those narrative lines.

[CH15-DIALOGUE-001] Pass if each revised Peter exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:115, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:213, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:367 includes at least one line of control language that is not financial/accounting-coded and still sounds period-authentic; fail if all three exchanges remain dominated by bookkeeping diction alone.

[CH15-PROSE-001] Pass if the revised chapter retains no more than two 'as if' constructions total in the cited cluster and each removed instance is replaced with concrete sensory or behavioral detail visible on the page; fail if the same comparison scaffold still appears three or more times in those revised beats.

[FBR-011] Pass if the hospital scene no longer stacks multiple near-identical bookkeeping assertions and instead shows at least two distinct control tactics beyond receipts/accounting language. Fail if Peter's dialogue remains predominantly repetitive fiscal bookkeeping rhetoric.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, on reread of the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:213, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:363, each confrontation turns on a different leverage point and produces a different immediate outcome; fail if two or more exchanges still hinge on the same cost/obligation argument.
findings.problem: The core Peter-versus-Noreen confrontation is restated three times in near-identical debt/obligation terms, so scene-level leverage changes too slowly and the chapter's middle reads as reiteration instead of escalation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Restructure the confrontations at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:213, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:363 so each beat introduces a distinct coercive mechanism (hospital access, household resource control, and personal boundary breach) with a new consequence, rather than re-arguing the same cost ledger premise.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:159 contains explicit subject-plus-finite-verb sentence structure (outside quoted lists/dialogue); fail if stand-alone clipped fragments remain in those narrative lines.
findings.problem: Narration repeatedly uses clipped fragments (e.g., stand-alone noun phrases) as pressure shorthand, which violates the constitution's anti-elliptical-clipping rule and weakens syntactic control at key emotional beats.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:159, replace clipped narrative fragments with grammatically complete sentences that keep the same sensory detail and pace.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each revised Peter exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:115, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:213, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:367 includes at least one line of control language that is not financial/accounting-coded and still sounds period-authentic; fail if all three exchanges remain dominated by bookkeeping diction alone.
findings.problem: Peter's dialogue over-literalizes his style-bible lexical signature (records/cost/obligation/accounting) so persistently that voice precision drifts into mechanical repetition and flattens scene unpredictability.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Peter's lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:115, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:213, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:227, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:231, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:367 by retaining some formal control diction but adding non-ledger pressure language (status fear, custody anxiety, social image, bodily disgust) so his control strategy varies by moment.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter retains no more than two 'as if' constructions total in the cited cluster and each removed instance is replaced with concrete sensory or behavioral detail visible on the page; fail if the same comparison scaffold still appears three or more times in those revised beats.
findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same 'as if' comparison scaffold more than ten times, creating a detectable sentence tic and flattening prose rhythm where sharper direct perception would carry more force.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:103, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:207, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:371, replace repeated 'as if' comparisons with direct action, physical sensation, or concrete social observation in Noreen-close POV.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the hospital scene no longer stacks multiple near-identical bookkeeping assertions and instead shows at least two distinct control tactics beyond receipts/accounting language. Fail if Peter's dialogue remains predominantly repetitive fiscal bookkeeping rhetoric.
findings.problem: Same overfitting pattern in hospital aftermath: receipts/files/order language is repeated so heavily that Peter's voice stops escalating and starts looping.
findings.rewrite_direction: At `snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5648`, `snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5654`, and `snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5672`, KEEP the file prop once but REPLACE at least two repeated accounting turns with fresh coercive moves specific to hospital context (access mediation, message control, reputation leverage with staff).

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the more specific and potentially uncomfortable rendering if it is character-true and causally necessary. Intimacy, bodily risk, and moral compromise must be shown with concrete detail and aftermath, then integrated into later behavior rather than quarantined as a one-scene shock.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a draft abstracts pain, sex, coercion, blood, or economic shame at the exact moment consequence should be embodied, revise by replacing summary with physically and socially specific action tied to POV stakes.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High by default for spoken lines; uncontracted forms are marked choices for period formality, emphasis, or character-specific control behavior.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural engines across adjacent chapters: ensemble social scenes, inventory chapters, information-turn chapters, acted-upon pressure chapters, and aftermath chapters. Vary scene count (2 to 4), dialogue density, and interiority depth so consecutive chapters do not share the same pacing signature.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-authentic but readable contemporary English, favoring concrete nouns and precise verbs over lyrical abstraction.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating primarily between Elsie and Noreen, with occasional tightly limited deviations only when causal clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Sentence rhythm should alternate compressed pressure bursts with longer observational lines that carry social texture; avoid monotone paragraph cadence.
